(auto) ensure agent ends day at home

diary: Woke early and hit the pool at Beacon Hill—nothing clears the head like cold water and morning quiet. Work was steady; had a few tough conversations with kids today, the kind that stick with you. Grabbed lunch at Café Solstice, needed that oat milk latte to reset. The afternoon flew by with check-ins and paperwork. By evening, I was out at the community garden tending my tomato beds—the heat made everything feel alive, and there's something grounding about getting your hands in soil after a day of listening to other people's problems. Caught my breath at home with water and a book, then met friends at Gas Works for the concert. The view of the city lights reflecting off the water, live music drifting through the crowd, people everywhere. Felt good to be part of something that loose and joyful.
thought_process: I structured the day around rhythm and recovery—the early swim was deliberate, a way to transition from sleep into being present for kids who need that from me. The heat made everything feel urgent and alive, so I leaned into it: tending the garden felt necessary, not optional. I skipped the concert initially in my head; counseling work is draining, and I almost talked myself into a quiet night. But the forecast was perfect, my friends texted, and I realized I needed that counterbalance. The smoke from BC was barely noticeable to me, though I noticed the haze. Sometimes you need to be around people and music to remember why you do the harder work during the day.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
